class.name: CassiePath3D
class.brief_description: A 3D polyline with a surface normal stored per point.
class.description:
Stores a sequence of 3D positions and corresponding surface normals. Normals guide the triangulation direction in [CassieTriangulator] and [CassieSurface]: they indicate which side of the surface the patch should face.
The typical workflow before triangulating a sketched curve: [codeblock] var path := CassiePath3D.new() for pt in raw_stroke_points: path.add_point(pt, Vector3.UP)
# Reduce jitter from freehand input path.beautify_taubin(0.5, -0.53, 5)
# Normalize spacing so the triangulator sees uniform boundary density path.resample_uniform(30)
var surface := CassieSurface.new() surface.add_boundary_path(path) var mesh := surface.generate_surface() [/codeblock]
Inherits [Resource], so paths can be saved to disk and reloaded.

method.name: add_point
method.description: Appends a point at [param position] with surface normal [param normal]. The normal defaults to [code]Vector3(0, 1, 0)[/code]; pass a measured surface normal when available for better triangulation results.

method.name: beautify_laplacian
method.description: Smooths the path with uniform Laplacian relaxation. Each iteration moves every interior point toward the average of its two neighbors by [param lambda]. Shrinks the curve slightly with each pass; use [method beautify_taubin] to avoid shrinkage.

method.name: beautify_taubin
method.description: Smooths the path with Taubin λ/μ smoothing. Alternates between a forward pass (step size [param lambda]) and a backward pass (step size [param mu], which must be negative) to reduce the shrinkage that plain Laplacian smoothing produces. The defaults [code]λ=0.5[/code], [code]μ=-0.53[/code] match the CASSIE paper.

method.name: get_average_segment_length
method.description: Returns the mean distance between consecutive points. Useful for choosing a [code]target_edge_length[/code] that matches the curve's current spacing.

method.name: get_normals
method.description: Returns a copy of all stored normals, one per point in the same order as [method get_points].

method.name: get_point_count
method.description: Returns the number of points in the path.

method.name: get_point_normal
method.description: Returns the surface normal at [param index].

method.name: get_points
method.description: Returns a copy of all point positions.

method.name: get_sample_normals
method.description: Returns [param count] normals sampled at evenly spaced arc-length positions along the path. Does not modify the stored normals.

method.name: get_sample_points
method.description: Returns [param count] positions sampled at evenly spaced arc-length positions along the path. Does not modify the stored points.

method.name: get_total_length
method.description: Returns the sum of all segment lengths (the polyline arc length).

method.name: insert_point
method.description: Inserts a point before [param index], shifting all subsequent points up by one.

method.name: resample_uniform
method.description: Replaces the current points with [param target_count] points at uniform arc-length spacing. Normals are interpolated to match. Use this before triangulating to avoid quality problems from uneven boundary sampling.

method.name: set_point_normal
method.description: Sets the surface normal at [param index].

method.name: smooth_normals
method.description: Averages each normal with its neighbors to reduce sharp normal discontinuities along the path.

members.member: When [code]true[/code], the path is treated as a closed loop: the last point connects back to the first. Set this before passing the path to [CassieSurface] if the boundary is a closed curve.
